propositions: A completion PASS currently persists no durable per-criterion record, unlike plan and code reviews, because findings are emitted only for failing criteria.
The verifier output contract gains a positive per-criterion criteria[] array with {criterion, met, citation, kind} entries alongside the failures-only findings.
kind is a closed two-value set {codebase-verifiable, operator-attested} distinguishing source-verified criteria from externally attested evidence.
A PASS emits a completion_verifier_pass_v1 sidecar under a new SCHEMA_PASS constant while the existing FAIL SCHEMA remains unchanged and untouched.
reconcile_verdict must not flip a PASS carrying a populated criteria[] with empty findings to FAIL, and criteria must survive reconcile end-to-end.
criteria[] is optional and additive, so older agents omitting it retain today's PASS-counts-only behavior with no backfill.
